actionLog.details: Technician arranges workspace, lays out tools including torque wrench, calipers, and bore gauge on clean bench surface
Reads data plate on HPC-7 hydraulic pump — P/N 881700-1089, S/N SN-2024-11432
Removes four mounting bolts from pump housing using 9/16 socket
Visual inspection of main housing bore — notes minor scoring on inner surface, no corrosion
Measures bore diameter with inside micrometer — reading 2.4985 inches, within service limits per CMM Table 5-3
Removes worn main shaft seal using seal pick — notes seal lip hardening and minor extrusion
Cleans bore and seal groove with approved solvent, compressed air dry
Installs new main shaft seal P/N 65-1234-7, lubricated with MIL-PRF-83282 hydraulic fluid
Reinstalls pump housing, torques mounting bolts to 85 in-lbs per CMM section 6.4
Connects to test stand, applies 3000 psi system pressure — no leaks observed after 5-minute hold
Records test results, signs work order

anomalies.description: Minor scoring observed on housing bore inner surface — within serviceable limits per CMM Table 5-3, no corrective action required
Main shaft seal shows hardening and lip extrusion consistent with heat exposure — replaced

audioTranscript:
[00:00] Alright, starting the overhaul on the HPC-7 pump. Part number 881700-1089, serial number Sierra November 2024-11432.
[00:42] Pulling the four mounting bolts now... housing is free.
[01:18] Looking at the bore... I can see some light scoring, nothing deep. Let me measure it.
[01:45] Bore reads 2.4985 inches. That's within limits per Table 5-3, service limit is 2.5010.
[02:12] This seal is definitely shot — you can see the lip is hardened and starting to extrude. Classic heat damage. Pulling it out.
[02:38] New seal going in — part number 65-1234-7. Lubing it up with 83282.
[03:30] Torquing the housing bolts to 85 inch-pounds. One... two... three... four. All good.
[04:05] Connecting to the test stand now. Running at 3000 psi... holding... no leaks. Five-minute hold is good.
[04:40] Pump passes functional test. Serviceable, ready for release.

photoExtractions.description: Data plate on HPC-7 pump housing
Worn main shaft seal removed from pump

extractedData.condition: Hardened lip with extrusion, heat damage evident

photoExtractions.description: Test gauge during 3000 PSI pressure hold
